preamble: The retraction or depalatalization of a voiceless palatal stop back to a voiceless velar stop is less common but can occur in dialectal simplification or historical mergers.

examples.note: Observed in various languages as a simplification of the palatal stop to the more common velar stop.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
